sections.paragraph:
(“father of the king,” or “father king”), [Abimelech], pr.n.-

(1) of several kings in the land of the Philistines, living at different periods, Gen 20:2, sqq.; 21:22 , sqq.; 26 Gen 21:26:l, sqq.; Psa 34:1. As the same king, who in the Ps. loc. cit. is called Abimelech, is in 1Sa 21:11, called Achish (אָכִישׁ), this name or title appears to have been mostly common to them, like داد شاه Padishah (father king) of the Persian kings, and اتاليق Atalik (father, properly paternity) of the Khans of Bokhara.-(2) a son of Gideon, Jud. 8:31 , seq.; 9:1 , seq.; 2Sa 11:21.-(3) 1Ch 18:16, where indeed we probably should read אֲחִימֶלֶךְ, as 2Sa 8:17.
